channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Rivers senator faults NNPCL over repeated absence from crude theft probe
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A Rivers senator criticises NNPCL for repeatedly missing crude theft probe meetings, hindering legislative oversight and transparency efforts.

item.title: Safe Schools Initiative Yields Results As Police Nab Seven Over Gadau Hostel Robbery
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Bauchi State Police Command has said its Safe Schools Initiative recorded another success with the arrest of seven suspected armed robbers linked to an attack on students residing in an off-campus hostel in Gadau. The Police command, in a statement signed by its spokesman, SP Nafiu Habib, said p

item.title: How we mistakenly shot at policemen in Lagos — Suspect
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Forty-five-year-old Tosin Agbeyangi, a popular caterer in the Oshodi area of Lagos State, has narrated how his gang unknowingly opened fire... The post How we mistakenly shot at policemen in Lagos — Suspect appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Ododo Reaffirms No-Ransom Policy As Security Forces Rescue Abducted NECO Candidates In Kogi
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Kogi State governor, Alhaji Ahmed Usman Ododo, has reiterated his administration’s policy of not negotiating with kidnappers or paying ransom, following the successful rescue of four persons abducted from a NECO examination centre in Dekina Local Government Area of the state. The governor made

item.title: 20 Schoolchildren, 1 Adult Killed In Ugandan Bus Crash
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: A bus carrying schoolchildren returning from a trip veered off the road in eastern Uganda on Friday, killing 20 pupils and one adult, police have confirmed. The vehicle, belonging to King David Junior School in Kampala, was coming back from a school excursion to Sipi Falls in Kapchorwa District when

item.title: China, Russia, 28 others sign agreement for World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organization
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Twenty-nine nations have entered into an agreement in Shanghai, China, to establish the World Artificial Intelligence Cooperation Organization, known as WAICO. During the World AI Conference in Shanghai, Chinese leader, Xi Jinping declared that China will offer 5,000 opportunities for AI training an

item.title: FG Launches $1.07bn Health, Governance Programmes
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Federal Government has launched $1.07bn health and governance programmes aimed at strengthening primary healthcare, improving public sector governance and accelerating human capital development across the country. President Bola Tinubu unveiled the initiatives on Thursday at the State House, Abu

item.title: DSS, foreign observers to monitor NBA election – Osigwe
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The President of the Nigerian Bar Association, NBA, Mazi Afam Osigwe, SAN, has said that the July 18, 2026 National Election of the association would be monitored by the Department of State Services, DSS, and foreign observers to ensure the integrity of the poll. Osigwe said the security o

item.title: Moghalu commiserates with Amaechi over mother’s death
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The former Managing Director of the National Inland Waterways Authority (NIWA), Dr. George Moghalu, has commiserated with the vice presidential candidate of African Democratic Congress (ADC) Rt. Hon. Rotimi Amaechi, over the death of his mother, Dame Ezinne Mary Oduah Amaechi. The post Moghalu commi

item.title: Oil Market Can’t Live Without Hormuz Barrels for Long
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Flows through the critical waterway haven’t fully stopped, but signs of stress in the market are mounting.

item.title: Troops foil bandits’ attack, recover 147 rustled animals along Kano-Katsina border

item.description: Troops of the Joint Task Force (JTF) Kano have foiled a bandits' attack and recovered 147 rustled animals during... The post Troops foil bandits’ attack, recover 147 rustled animals along Kano-Katsina border appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: US Bans Entry Of Foreigners Linked To Far-Left Terrorist Groups
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The United States Secretary of State Marco Rubio has announced new visa restrictions barring foreign nationals accused of financing, inciting, aiding or abetting what the administration describes as far-left terrorist groups. Rubio announced the policy on Thursday, saying the measures were aimed at

item.title: Five Women's Super League fixtures live on Sky Sports including Grand Final
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Sky Sports' live coverage of the Women's Super League has been confirmed, with a blockbuster Grand Final rematch between St Helens and Wigan Warriors launching a run of five live fixtures during the second half of the 2026 season.

item.title: Innolight Gets Approval for Up to $8 Billion Hong Kong Listing
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Zhongji Innolight Co. received approval from China’s securities regulator and the Hong Kong stock exchange for what is likely to be one of the city’s biggest listings in years.

item.title: Keyamo okays third-party payment of Obi’s N25,000 airport fine
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Minister of Aviation and Aerospace Development, Mr Festus Keyamo, has asked Mr Nnaemeka Ikerionwu, who volunteered to pay the N25,000 fine imposed on Labour Party, LP, presidential candidate in 2023, Mr Peter Obi, to liaise with the Federal Airports Authority of Nigeria, FAAN, on the payment process

item.title: NBA invites DSS, ICPC to monitor national officers’ elections
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) has reaffirmed its resolve to proceed with its 2026 national officers' election on Saturday. The post NBA invites DSS, ICPC to monitor national officers’ elections appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Why the first GPU financiers are turning to inference chips in a $400 million deal
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: A $400 million chip-backed loan points to the next wave of AI infrastructure deals.

item.title: Pep's prediction correct as Rodri returns to dominant best
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Rodri came in for some criticism in Spain after they were held to a goalless draw by Cape Verde in their first game of the World Cup. There were accusations that he had moved the ball too slowly; that he was in some way responsible for the result.

item.title: How Dangote’s return to dollar sales affects Nigerian fuel market
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Dangote refinery’s third pivot to dollar pricing within 16 months is testing whether Nigeria’s naira-for-crude policy can survive the commercial logic of its single largest beneficiary.

item.dc:creator: YNaija
item.description: The Nigerian presidential elections for 2027 are drawing closer, and it is important for Nigerians to know the candidates vying for the presidential seat. This follows the club’s dismal performance in the 2025/2026 Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL) season, which culminated in its relegati

In a st

item.title: Ghanaian TikToker Jailed For False Ritual Claims Against President Mahama

item.description: A Ghanaian court has sentenced TikToker Camilla Alhassan to one year in prison after she pleaded guilty to publishing false claims that President John Mahama sacrificed 32 cows in a ritual to secure victory in Ghana’s 2024 presidential election. According to the BBC on Friday, the 43-year-old

item.title: Why Celebrity-Owned Brands Are Outperforming Traditional Businesses

item.description: Celebrity brands have moved well past the era of licensing deals and endorsements. item.title: Two women are leading Nigeria’s first insure-fintech — meet the team behind Skydd

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Bala Wunti Support Organisation (BWSO) has announced plans to institutionalise an annual celebration of the humanitarian and philanthropic contributions of Dr Bala Maijama’a Wunti through the inauguration of a committee for the maiden Bala Wunti Global Impact Day. The event, billed for Aug

item.title: Court Strikes Out Amaechi’s Suit Challenging Atiku’s ADC Presidential Candidacy

item.title: The young Kenyan engineer who thinks robots belong in every classroom
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: During her mentoring of young people in STEM, she met deaf students struggling through STEM classes because qualified sign language interpreters were scarce. It struck her as an engineering problem as much as an educational one.

Burnham’s confirmation on Friday follows Starmer’s resignation in June after mounting political pressure over declining

item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [CPJ] Government allied Wazalendo militiamen have threatened to kill or capture one reporter and to burn down another's radio station because of unfavorable coverage, as frontlines shift under diplomatic pressure for peace in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo.

item.title: Project 'Afrika’: Inside Russia's propaganda machine
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: After a leak of confidential documents revealed how Russian agents have been placing hundreds of articles in French-speaking media outlets in West and Central Africa, FRANCE 24 travelled to Dakar, Senegal, to investigate this secret operation.

item.title: Reliance Founders Lift Stake by Most Since 2019 as Stock Slumps
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Reliance Industries Ltd. founders, led by billionaire Mukesh Ambani, raised stake in the Indian oil-to-telecom conglomerate by the most in nearly seven years following the stock’s prolonged slump in the wake of the US-Iran war.

item.description: Analysts say the goals range from targeting Iran's supply lines to curbing its influence in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Lula’s Panda-Bond Push Tests China’s Appeal for Brazil’s Firms
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Brazil has big ambitions for its plan to enter China’s onshore bond market — if only corporate Brazil follows along.

item.title: Clean Energy Startups Jostle For $1m All On Off-grid Energy Challenge
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigerian clean energy startups are competing for All On 2026 $1 million Off-Grid Energy Challenge. The challenge, which is a nationwide competition aimed at accelerating innovative solutions to expand electricity access to millions of underserved Nigerians, kicked off on July 2. The programme target

item.title: Top 10 most affordable states to live in Nigeria in June 2026
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Below are the 10 states that recorded the lowest all-items (headline) inflation rates in the country, making them the states where cost-of-living pressures were least pronounced during the month The post Top 10 most affordable states to live in Nigeria in June 2026 appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: 8-Year-Old British-Cameroonian Author Sets Guinness World Record
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Eight-year-old British child author of Cameroonian heritage, Jayce Joyce, has been recognised by Guinness World Records as the world’s youngest male editor of a published anthology, adding another remarkable achievement to his growing list of literary milestones. Jayce earned the record at jus

item.title: 2027: Kebbi Govt raises alarm over alleged fake news campaign
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Kebbi State Government has raised concerns over what it described as an emerging campaign of misinformation and fake news allegedly being orchestrated by opposition elements ahead of the 2027 general elections. The Commissioner for Information and Culture, Yakubu Ahmed, raised the concern during

item.title: ADC primary: Amaechi withdraws suit against Atiku
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja on Friday struck out a pre-election dispute suit filed by the former governor of Rivers State, Rotimi Amaechi, against ex-Vice President Atiku Abubakar. Amaechi had filed the suit challenging the emergence of Abubakar as the presidential candidate of the Afr

item.title: Teniola Aladese Leads Suspenseful Drama in Teaser for “We Must Begin Again, Together”
item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: The official teaser for "We Must Begin Again, Together" introduces a gripping drama starring Teniola Aladese. Directed by Adé Sultan Sangodoyin and produced by Melissa Adeyemo, the film tracks a woman's world upending after uncovering a devastating double betrayal in a conservative town. The post Te

item.title: ‘Possible but harder’: Ballon d’Or says players can win award outside Europe
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Ballon d'Or has dismissed the long-held belief that footballers must play for a European club to win the sport's most prestigious individual honour, insisting there is no such requirement under the current rules. The post ‘Possible but harder’: Ballon d’Or says players can win

item.title: 🔴 UK’s Andy Burnham named Labour leader ahead of taking over as PM
item.dc:creator: France 24

item.title: BREAKING: Ododo Receives Rescued Dekina Kidnap Victims at Kogi Govt House
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Kogi State Governor, Usman Ododo, has received the school principal, a National Examinations Council (NECO) official, and two students rescued after they were abducted during an attack on an unauthorized examination centre in Olowa, Dekina Local Government Area. TVC News reports that the victims wer

item.title: Goodluck Jonathan Celebrates Son Ariwera’s Graduation From UK University
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former President Goodluck Jonathan has congratulated his son, Ariwera Jonathan, on his graduation from Manchester Metropolitan University, expressing pride in his academic achievement. Sharing the milestone in a heartfelt message, Jonathan said he and his wife were proud of Ariwera’s dedicatio

item.title: Two Women Are Leading Nigeria’s First Insure-Fintech – Meet the Team Behind Skydd
item.dc:creator: Techpoint Africa
item.description: For decades, protection has been treated as something Nigerians buy...

item.title: 'We will have to share the water': a quarter of small waterways in France have run dry
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – Friday, July 17: China reacts after the UK nationalises its last remaining virgin steel factory, expropriating Chinese Jingye Group. In other news, low rainfall in May followed by three intense heatwaves have 100 French departments under water restrictions. Also, in Central Asia, a te

item.title: Serie A transfer: Monza and Inter Milan working out deal for Super Eagles star
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: AC Monza have stepped up their efforts to sign Nigerian midfielder Ebenezer Akinsanmiro from Inter Milan as the two Serie A clubs continue talks over a permanent transfer, Soccernet.ng reports. According to reports from Sky Sport Italia through transfer expert Gianluca Di Marzio, negotiations are pr

item.title: Eight cattle killed, many injured in fresh Plateau attack
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Gunmen on Thursday afternoon killed eight cattle and injured several others in a fresh attack in Yarwe village, Fan District of Barkin Ladi Local Government Area of Plateau State. State Chairman of Miyetti Allah Cattle Breeders Association of Nigeria (MACBAN), Ibrahim Yusuf Babayo, who confirmed the
